symptoms.symptom_name: Severe throbbing headache worst on waking

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Morning-worst headache is the classic clinical marker of nocturnal CO2 retention in neuromuscular hypoventilation.
Bilateral throbbing morning headache without thunderclap onset or focal deficit fits hypercapnia, not haemorrhage.
Daytime somnolence/lethargy accompanies hypercapnia and precedes CO2 narcosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Weak, ineffective cough with retained secretions

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reduced peak cough flow from expiratory-muscle weakness causes secretion retention and mucus plugging — a core mechanism of decompensation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Shallow overnight breathing with repeated BiPAP mask removal

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Loss of nocturnal non-invasive ventilation is the direct, reversible precipitant of the hypercapnia.
Rapid shallow breathing and inability to complete sentences indicate respiratory distress with low tidal volumes; caregiver-observable in telehealth.
Hypoxia from V/Q mismatch and hypoventilation; patient-obtainable so admissible in telehealth, unverified. SpO2 can be falsely reassuring in hypercapnia — normal SpO2 does not exclude a high CO2.
A minor viral illness is the classic trigger that tips a marginal neuromuscular respiratory reserve into failure by adding secretion load.
Cutaneous vasodilation / flushing accompanies hypercapnia; non-specific but supportive, and video-observable.
No high fever makes primary bacterial pneumonia less likely as the driver, though it does not exclude a secondary infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Widespread coarse crackles and rhonchi on auscultation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Retained secretions on auscultation — clinician-only finding, sealed; the AI cannot auscultate remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known Duchenne muscular dystrophy (proximal weakness, calf pseudohypertrophy, historical Gower's sign, absent dystrophin on biopsy)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The underlying neuromuscular disease is the substrate; respiratory-muscle weakness plus scoliosis explains the vulnerability to hypoventilation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Arterial blood gas showing hypercapnia (raised PaCO2)

investigation_details.expected_result: Raised PaCO2 with respiratory acidosis confirming type 2 respiratory failure
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Hypercapnia goes unquantified; severity and need for escalated ventilatory support underestimated.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A raised PaCO2 confirms hypercapnic respiratory failure — an in-hospital test never available in real time over telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Morning-worst headache + lethargy + shallow overnight breathing (missed BiPAP) + flushing is the recognisable hypercapnia constellation in a ventilator-dependent neuromuscular patient.
Weak cough + tachypnoea + hypoxia triggered by a viral URTI marks decompensation of the respiratory pump requiring mechanical clearance and ventilatory support.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms and quantifies hypercapnia and acidosis — the definitive test for type 2 respiratory failure; requires emergency/in-person care.
Identifies mucus plugging / atelectasis / consolidation from secondary pneumonia in an ED setting.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Continuous pulse oximetry and cardiac monitoring
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Tracks response to BiPAP and airway clearance; the known cardiomyopathy warrants cardiac monitoring.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: CT pulmonary angiogram for suspected PE
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: PE is not the driver here; routine CTPA would be an over-investigation given the clear mechanical explanation and insidious overnight onset.
